The Meg (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $145,443,742Details
International Box Office $383,800,000
Worldwide Box Office $529,243,742
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $21,937,226
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $15,450,410
Total $37,387,636
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A deep-sea submersible—part of an international undersea observation program—is attacked by a massive creature, previously thought to be extinct, and now lies disabled at the bottom of the deepest trench in the Pacific…with its crew trapped inside. With time running out, expert deep sea rescue diver Jonas Taylor is recruited by a visionary Chinese oceanographer, against the wishes of his daughter Suyin, to save the crew—and the ocean itself—from this unstoppable threat: a pre-historic 75-foot-long shark known as the Megalodon. What no one could have imagined is that, years before, Taylor had encountered this same terrifying creature. Now, teamed with Suyin, he must confront his fears and risk his own life to save everyone trapped below…bringing him face to face once more with the greatest and largest predator of all time.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$45,402,195 (31.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.20 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:27.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$178,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,118 opening theaters/4,118 max. theaters, 6.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $190,944,809

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 10th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
August 10th, 2018 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: August 9th, 2018 (Wide) (China)
August 9th, 2018 (Wide), released as Shark - Il primo squalo (Italy)
August 10th, 2018 (Wide) (Netherlands)
August 10th, 2018 (Wide), released as MEG: Hrozba z hlbín (Slovakia)
August 10th, 2018 (Wide) (Thailand)
Video Release: November 13th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for action/peril, bloody images and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2500 (Cert #51305), 11/1/2017)
Running Time: 113 minutes
Franchise: The Meg
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad, Dishonorable Discharge, Exes in Love, Redemption, Rescue, Underwater
Subgenre: Action Horror
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Gravity Pictures, Flagship Entertainment, Apelles Entertainment, di Bonaventura Pictures, Maeday Productions, On a Roll Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A deep-sea submersible—part of an international undersea observation program—is attacked by a massive creature, previously thought to be extinct, and now lies disabled at the bottom of the deepest trench in the Pacific…with its crew trapped inside. With time running out, expert deep sea rescue diver Jonas Taylor is recruited by a visionary Chinese oceanographer, against the wishes of his daughter Suyin, to save the crew—and the ocean itself—from this unstoppable threat: a pre-historic 75-foot-long shark known as the Megalodon. What no one could have imagined is that, years before, Taylor had encountered this same terrifying creature. Now, teamed with Suyin, he must confront his fears and risk his own life to save everyone trapped below…bringing him face to face once more with the greatest and largest predator of all time.
